steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows "in several ______ exchanging information with state geological surveys" and must connect "several ways" to a list of three parallel by-phrases. The punctuation must introduce a list after a complete independent clause.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ways. By: The period ends the sentence, leaving "By exchanging information, linking maps, and maintaining a database" as a fragment with no main verb. ✗
  • Choice B — ways: by: The clause before the colon is complete and independent. The colon correctly signals the elaborating list of three by-phrases that follows. ✓
  • Choice C — ways; by: A semicolon must join two independent clauses. What follows is a list of phrases, not an independent clause, so the semicolon is wrong here. ✗
  • Choice D — ways by:: A colon cannot follow a preposition. This is ungrammatical — "several ways by:" cannot introduce anything. ✗
B is correct.
